entry.title: image
consortium /kənˈsɔːrtiəm/
entry.content: a group of people, countries, companies, etc. who are working together on a particular project

entry.title: dissertation /ˌdɪsərˈteɪʃn/
entry.content:
doctoral dissertation 博士论文
academic dissertation 学位论文

entry.title: evaluative adj
entry.content:
seminal /ˈsemɪnl/
very important and having a strong influence on later developments
useful
timely
detailed
thorough
excellent
influential
important
innovative
pioneering
impressive
wide-ranging
comprehensive
ground-breaking

entry.title: determine /dɪˈtɜːrmɪn/
entry.content:
• to discover the facts about sth; to calculate sth exactly
    An inquiry was set up to determine the cause of the accident.

    We set out to determine exactly what happened that night.

  • 决定性因素
    determining factors

  • to officially decide or arrange sth
    A date for the meeting has yet to be determined.

  • to decide definitely to do sth
    They determined to start early.

entry.title: underlying /ˌʌndərˈlaɪɪŋ/
entry.content:
• important in a situation but not always easily noticed or stated clearly

The underlying assumption is that the amount of money available is limited.

entry.title: discourse /ˈdɪskɔːrs/
entry.content:
• a long and serious treatment or discussion of a subject in speech or writing

discoursed power 话语权
discourses studies 语篇研究

entry.title: adequate /ˈædɪkwət/
entry.content:
• enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need

an adequate supply of hot water
The room was small but adequate.

辨析 enough， sufficient， adequate

entry.title: ascertain /ˌæsərˈteɪn/
entry.content:
• to find out the true or correct information about sth

It can be difficult to ascertain the facts.
I ascertained that the driver was not badly hurt.

entry.title: differentiate /ˌdɪfəˈrenʃieɪt/
entry.content:
• = distinguish

辨析 differ， differentiate

Being cautious when writing about the future

Severe weather will almost certainly become more common in the future.

There is a strong/small possibility/chance that the situation will improve in the future.

Being cautious about writing recommendations.

Strategies to enhance X might involve …
Other types of response could include: a), b). …
These results would seem to suggest that the …
There would therefore seem to be a definite need for …
A reasonable approach to tackle this issue could be to …
Another possible area of future research would be to investigate why …

These findings cannot be extrapolated to all patients.
These data must be interpreted with caution because …
These results therefore need to be interpreted with caution.
These results do not rule out the influence of other factors in …
This account must be approached with some caution because …
It is important to bear in mind the possible bias in these responses.
Although exclusion of X did not …, these results should be interpreted with caution.
However, with a small sample size, caution must be applied, as the findings might not be …

entry.title: 表揭示
entry.content:
1. show
  2. illustrate
    • make the meaning of sth clearer by using examples, pictures
    • to show that sth is true or that a situation exists
    • (passive) to use pictures, photographs, diagrams, etc. in a book

  3. support the view

  4. confirm the importance

  5. demonstrate

    • to show or prove sth clearly

    The study demonstrates the link between poverty and malnutrition.

  6. reveal

  7. report

entry.title: discriminate /dɪˈskrɪməneɪt/
entry.content:
• to treat a person or group differently from another in an unfair way 歧视
  • to recognize a difference things 区别

entry.title: empirical /ɪmˈpɪrɪkəl/
entry.content:
based on scientific testing or practical experience, not on ideas

相关词汇
theoretical
hypothetical

entry.title: Acute respiratory infectious disease
entry.content:
急性呼吸道传染病

acute /əˈkjuːt/

  • very serious or sever
    the world's acute environmental problems
    competition for jobs is acute.
  • an acute illness is one that has quickly become severe and dangerous

respiratory /ˈrespərətɔːri/
connected with breathing

entry.title: address
entry.content:
(formal) to think about a problem or a situation and decide how you are going to deal with it

address something Your essay does not address the real issues.
address yourself to something We must address ourselves to the problem of traffic pollution.

entry.title: orthogonal /ɔr'θɑgənəl/
entry.content:
adjective
正交的
be orthogonal to each other
orthogonal array 正交数组
perpendicular 垂直的

entry.title: recursive /rɪˈkɜːrsɪv/
entry.content:
adjective
循环的
recursive algorithm 递归算法

entry.title: frontier /frʌnˈtɪr/
entry.content:
• the limit of sth, especially the limit of what is known about a particular subject or activity

    to push back the frontiers of science (= to increase knowledge of science)

    to roll back the frontiers of government (= to limit the powers of the government)

  • a line that separated two countries, etc; the land near this line
    frontier (between A and B)
    frontier (with sth)
    a frontier town/zone/post

entry.title: parabolic
entry.content:
parabolic 抛物线的
hyperbolic 双曲线的/语言夸张的
hyperbole
